categories.name: File Reference Validation
categories.description: Validates that file paths referenced in context files exist in the project.

categories.name: Build/Script Validation
categories.description: Validates that build and test commands referenced in context files match project scripts.

categories.name: Freshness Detection
categories.description: Detects context files that haven't been updated while referenced code has changed.

categories.name: Context Window Budget
categories.description: Monitors context file size to help teams manage context window consumption.

categories.name: Tier-Aware Token Accounting
categories.description: Reports per-section token cost for always-loaded context files and flags demotion candidates for on-demand tiers (skills, subagents, memory).

categories.name: Inferable Content
categories.description: Detects content that agents can already infer from project metadata.

categories.name: Cross-File Conflicts
categories.description: Detects conflicting directives across multiple context files.

categories.name: Client Metadata Validation
categories.description: Validates YAML frontmatter required by specific AI coding clients.

categories.name: CI Workflow Documentation
categories.description: Checks that release/deploy CI workflows are documented in context files.

categories.name: CI Secrets Documentation
categories.description: Checks that CI secrets used in workflows are documented in context files.

rules.description: Referenced file or directory does not exist at the specified path.
rules.trigger: Path reference in context file resolves to a non-existent file or directory.
rules.fixDescription: Replace broken path with suggestion from git rename detection or fuzzy matching.

rules.description: Glob pattern matches zero files in the project.
rules.trigger: Path contains wildcard characters (*, ?) but matches no files.

rules.description: Referenced directory does not exist.
rules.trigger: Path ending with / does not resolve to an existing directory.

rules.description: Package manager script name is not found in package.json scripts.
rules.trigger: npm run, pnpm, yarn, or bun script reference does not match a key in package.json#scripts.

rules.description: Make target is not found in Makefile.
rules.trigger: make command references a target not declared in the project's Makefile.

rules.description: Make command used but no Makefile exists in the project.
rules.trigger: Context file references a make command but no Makefile is found at the project root.

rules.description: npx package is not in project dependencies.
rules.trigger: npx package is not in package.json dependencies/devDependencies and not in node_modules/.bin.

rules.description: Common development tool is not in project dependencies.
rules.trigger: Tool name (vitest, jest, eslint, prettier, tsc, etc.) not found in dependencies or node_modules/.bin.
rules.message: "{cmd}" — "{tool}" not found in dependencies or node_modules/.bin

rules.description: Context file documents an npm write operation (deprecate, unpublish, dist-tag, access, owner) as a local CLI command. Under WebAuthn-only 2FA (no TOTP authenticator), these commands return 422 because npm treats CLI web-auth tokens as not-2FA-authenticated for writes. The npm website settings UI is the only path that works; CI-driven workflows are the scalable alternative.
rules.trigger: Context file references `npm deprecate`, `npm unpublish`, `npm dist-tag add/rm/set`, `npm access grant/revoke/2fa`, or `npm owner add/rm` as a command.
rules.message: "{cmd}" — npm {op} requires a 2FA-authenticated session; CLI returns 422 under WebAuthn-only auth

rules.description: Context file has not been updated in 30+ days while referenced code has changed.
rules.trigger: File last modified 30+ days ago AND paths it references have git commits since.
rules.message: Last updated {days} days ago. {path} has {N} commits since.

rules.description: Context file has not been updated in 14-30 days while referenced code has changed.
rules.trigger: File last modified 14-30 days ago AND paths it references have git commits since.
rules.message: Last updated {days} days ago. {path} has {N} commits since.

rules.description: Single context file consumes excessive context window space.
rules.trigger: File uses 8000+ tokens (configurable).
rules.message: {N} tokens — consumes significant context window space

rules.description: Single context file is large.
rules.trigger: File uses 3000-7999 tokens (configurable).

rules.description: Reports per-file token count.
rules.trigger: File uses 1000+ tokens (configurable).

rules.description: Combined context files exceed recommended total token budget.
rules.trigger: All context files combined use 5000+ tokens (configurable) AND there are multiple files.

rules.description: Reports the heaviest top-level sections in an always-loaded context file and suggests demoting the largest to an on-demand tier (skill, subagent, or memory).
rules.trigger: An always-loaded context file (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, etc. — excluding path-scoped rules files) exceeds the tierBreakdown threshold (default 1000 tokens) and contains H1 or H2 sections.
rules.message: {N} tokens loaded every session — heaviest top-level section(s): ...

rules.description: Combined token cost of always-loaded context files exceeds the recommended budget.
rules.trigger: Two or more always-loaded files total more than the tierAggregate threshold (default 4000 tokens).
rules.message: {count} always-loaded files total {N} tokens — loaded every session

rules.description: An always-loaded file uses inviolable framing (NEVER / ALWAYS / DO NOT / MUST NOT) paired with a concrete command, but no corresponding PreToolUse hook or permissions.deny entry exists in settings.json. Rules in always-loaded files are advisory — without a hook the agent may still run the command.
rules.trigger: Line in an always-loaded file matches NEVER|ALWAYS|DO NOT|MUST NOT with a backticked command, and .claude/settings.json / ~/.claude/settings.json does not contain a matching PreToolUse hook or permissions.deny entry.
rules.message: Inviolable framing without a hook to back it up

rules.description: Context file mentions a technology that is already in package.json dependencies.
rules.trigger: File contains phrases like 'use React' or 'built with Express' and that package is in dependencies.
rules.message: "{tech}" is in package.json {depType} — agent can infer this

rules.description: Context file describes the location of a directory that the agent can discover by listing files.
rules.trigger: File says files 'are in' or 'go in' a directory that exists in the project.
rules.message: Directory "{dir}" exists and is discoverable — agent can find this by listing files

rules.description: Two context files have significant content overlap.
rules.trigger: Two files share 60%+ of their lines (by trimmed content, lines >10 chars).

rules.description: Two context files specify mutually exclusive options in the same category.
rules.trigger: File A says 'use Jest' while File B says 'use Vitest' (or similar conflicts across 8 categories).

rules.description: File format requires frontmatter but none is present.
rules.trigger: Cursor .mdc, Copilot instruction, or Windsurf rule file has no --- frontmatter block.

rules.description: A required or recommended frontmatter field is absent.
rules.trigger: Frontmatter exists but is missing a key field (description for Cursor, applyTo for Copilot, trigger for Windsurf).

rules.description: A frontmatter field has an invalid value.
rules.trigger: alwaysApply is not true/false, trigger is not a valid enum value, globs doesn't look like a pattern.

rules.description: File has frontmatter but no activation mechanism.
rules.trigger: Cursor .mdc has frontmatter with neither globs nor alwaysApply set.
rules.message: No activation field — rule may not be applied automatically

rules.description: Release/deploy CI workflow exists but no context file documents the process.
rules.trigger: .github/workflows/ contains release/deploy/publish workflow but no context file mentions the release process.
rules.message: Release workflow found but no context file documents the release process

rules.description: CI workflow references a secret not mentioned in any context file.
rules.trigger: ${{ secrets.NAME }} found in workflow YAML but NAME not mentioned in any context file. GitHub-provided secrets (GITHUB_TOKEN, etc.) are excluded.
rules.message: CI secret "{name}" is used in {workflow} but not mentioned in any context file
